item.title: Nawaqanitawase to start for NSW Blues in Game 3
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/07/2026 Mark Nawaqanitawase will line-up again for the NSW Blues when they face the Queensland Maroons in Game 3 of the State of Origin tonight.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 08/07/2026

Mark Nawaqanitawase will line-up again for the NSW Blues when they face the Queensland Maroons in Game 3 of the State of Origin tonight.

Nawaqanitawase was brought in by NSW coach Laurie Daley for Game 2 where he scored a try, and he will be starting on the wing.

Other big names included in the Blues squad for tonight include James Tedesco, Stephen Crichton, Mitchell Moses, Nathan Cleary, Payne Haas, Hudson Young, and Isaiah Yeo.

Meanwhile, the Maroons has the services Kalyn Ponga, Robert Toia, Jojo Fifita, Cameron Munster, Harry Grant, Kurt Capewell, Reece Walsh and Murray Tuilagi.

The Blues won Game 1, edging the Maroons 22-20, while the Maroons defeated the Blues in Game 2 44-24.

The Blues will face the Maroons at 10.05 tonight.

item.title: Switzerland beat Colombia on penalties 4-3 and will face Argentina in quarter-finals
item.description: Switzerland have booked a quarter-final clash against defending champions Argentina at the FIFA World Cup after beating Colombia 4-3 on penalties in their Round of 16 match. The match ended nil-all after full time and extra time, with both sides unable to find the back of the net.
item.content:encoded:
Switzerland have booked a quarter-final clash against defending champions Argentina at the FIFA World Cup after beating Colombia 4-3 on penalties in their Round of 16 match.

The match ended nil-all after full time and extra time, with both sides unable to find the back of the net.

Switzerland held their nerve in the penalty shootout to edge Colombia 4-3 and keep their World Cup campaign alive.

Meanwhile, Argentina staged a stunning comeback to beat Egypt 3-2 in their Round of 16 clash and captain Lionel Messi has also broken two World Cup records.

Messi now holds the record for the most goals scored by a player at the World Cup with 21, and has also become the first player to score in nine consecutive World Cup matches.

Meanwhile, the quarter-finals starts this Friday with France taking on Morocco at 8am, and Spain will face Belgium at 7am on Saturday.

On Sunday, Norway will take on England at 9am while Argentina will face Switzerland at 1pm.

item.title: Father’s rugby journey inspires 21-year-old Amy Peters
item.description: 21-year-old Suva Women’s Number 8 Amy Peters says her love for rugby was inspired by her father, former Tailevu and Suva player Lagi Peters, as she prepares for the upcoming Subrails Marama Championship. While speaking with fijivillage News, Peters, who is from Wailevu in Kadavu, says she grew up around rugby, and the sport naturally became part of her life.
item.content:encoded:
21-year-old Suva Women’s Number 8 Amy Peters says her love for rugby was inspired by her father, former Tailevu and Suva player Lagi Peters, as she prepares for the upcoming Subrails Marama Championship.

While speaking with fijivillage News, Peters, who is from Wailevu in Kadavu, says she grew up around rugby, and the sport naturally became part of her life.

She says her father’s involvement in rugby played a major role in developing her passion for the game.

Inse rt: Peters sharing about her father 7th July 26

Peters says her father played for Tailevu and Suva and was also believed to have been part of an extended Fiji squad.

The 21-year-old has been part of the Suva Women’s team since 2021.

Speaking about preparations for the upcoming tournament, Peters says the team has been working hard over the past few weeks, with a strong focus on improving their techniques.

She says the coaches have also played an important role in helping the players improve, and she believes preparations are progressing well.

On discipline, Peters says it comes down to how players prepare themselves for a match.

She says if players respect themselves and the game, they will also respect other players on the field.

Peters says the team has not faced many major challenges and thanked Suva supporters for their continued backing.

She hopes supporters will turn up at the ground and get behind the Suva Women’s team during the tournament.

The Skipper Cup and Subrails Marama Championship kicks off next month.

Meanwhile, the Ranadi Cup starts this Friday with Bua taking on Taveuni at 11am at Bua College, Yasawa will face Vatukoula at 11am at Churchill Park, Northland will play Ra at 11am at Ratu Cakobau Park, Coastland will meet Northern Bulls at 11am at Narauyaba Sports Ground, Navosa will meet Ovalau at 11am at Buckhurst Ground and Malolo will meet face Yasawa at 3pm at Lawaqa Park.

The Vanua Cup also kicks off this Friday with Bua taking on Macuata at Bua College, Yasawa will meet Vatukoula at Churchill Park, Northland will face Ra at Ratu Cakobau Park, Coastland will meet Northern Bulls at 11am at Narauyaba Sports Ground, Nasinu will take on Ovalau at Buckhurst Ground and Namosi will meet Serua at Thompson Park.

All matches are at 3pm with the U20 starting at 11am.

item.title: Messi breaks two World Cup records
item.description: Lionel Messi has broken two FIFA World Cup records after inspiring defending champions Argentina to a stunning 3-2 comeback victor

item.title: Defending champions Argentina beat Egypt 3-2 after stunning comeback
item.description: Defending champions Argentina have beaten Egypt 3-2 in their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash after staging a stunning comeback. Egypt had taken a 2-0 lead before Argentina fought their way back into the match.
item.content:encoded:
Defending champions Argentina have beaten Egypt 3-2 in their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash after staging a stunning comeback.

Egypt had taken a 2-0 lead before Argentina fought their way back into the match.

It is a heartbreaking loss for Egypt as they nearly pulled it off.

Defender Yasser Ibrahim scored for Egypt, while another Egyptian goal in the 58th minute was ruled out for a foul.

However, Mostafa Zico scored shortly afterwards to extend Egypt’s lead.

Argentina centre-back Cristian Romero pulled one back in the 79th minute after an assist from Lionel Messi.

Messi, who had earlier missed a penalty, scored in the 83rd minute to level the scores at 2-2.

Enzo Fernández then scored a goal to complete the comeback and secure a 3-2 victory.

Argentina have booked their place in the quarter-finals, where they will face either Switzerland or Colombia.

item.title: Queensland Maroons and NSW Blues locked in for State of Origin decider at 10.05pm tonight
item.description: The Queensland Maroons and the New South Wales Blues will go into tonight's State of Origin decider unchanged, with the series set to be decided before a sell-out crowd at Suncorp Stadium . The Blues have been boosted by the availability of Ethan Strange , who has recovered from an ankle injury after initially being placed in a protective boot during camp.
item.content:encoded:
The Queensland Maroons and the New South Wales Blues will go into tonight's State of Origin decider unchanged, with the series set to be decided before a sell-out crowd at Suncorp Stadium .

The Blues have been boosted by the availability of Ethan Strange , who has recovered from an ankle injury after initially being placed in a protective boot during camp.

His recovery allowed Matt Burton to return to Sydney after joining the squad as injury cover.

Queensland have also received good news, with forward Pat Carrigan declared fit despite recently recovering from ankle surgery.

The Maroons will be looking to repeat last year's comeback after losing Game One before winning the final two matches to claim the series.

Halfback Sam Walker has impressed since making his Origin debut and will again partner Cameron Munster , while Selwyn Cobbo , Hamiso Tabuai-Fidow and Kalyn Ponga provide Queensland with plenty of attacking firepower.

For New South Wales, debutant Jack Bostock comes into the side alongside the returning Liam Martin , while much of the focus will be on halfback Nathan Cleary , who is chasing his first State of Origin series-deciding victory.

Game 3 of the 2026 Men's State of Origin kicks off tonight at 10.05pm at Suncorp Stadium in Australia.

item.title: Eseta Waqaira replaces injured Kalesi Tawake in Fiji Pearls squad
item.description: Eseta Waqaira has been named as a replacement for Kalesi Tawake in the Fiji Pearls squad for the upcoming Spirit Series in Sydney. In a statement, the Fiji Netball Association thanked Tawake for her hard work, commitment, and dedication to the team and wished her a smooth and speedy recovery.
item.content:encoded:
Eseta Waqaira has been named as a replacement for Kalesi Tawake in the Fiji Pearls squad for the upcoming Spirit Series in Sydney.

In a statement, the Fiji Netball Association thanked Tawake for her hard work, commitment, and dedication to the team and wished her a smooth and speedy recovery.

Waqaira now joins the Fiji Pearls as they continue preparations for the Spirit Series in Sydney.

The Association has wished Waqaira all the best as she joins the Fiji Pearls, expressing confidence that she will embrace the opportunity and give her best in the upcoming Spirit Series.

The Navolau, Naitasiri player, plays goal defence and goal keeper.

item.title: 17-year-old Gianna Singh eyes Fiji and US representation as she chases golfing dream
item.description: At just 17 years old, Gianna Singh aspires to represent both Fiji and her country of birth, the United States, in golf and become the first female American Indo-Fijian golfer to be inducted into the World Golf Hall of Fame, as she prepares to compete in the U.S. Women’s Amateur Championship for the first time. Her journey began when her father, Edwin Singh, took her with him to the golf course aft
item.content:encoded:
At just 17 years old, Gianna Singh aspires to represent both Fiji and her country of birth, the United States, in golf and become the first female American Indo-Fijian golfer to be inducted into the World Golf Hall of Fame, as she prepares to compete in the U.S. Women’s Amateur Championship for the first time.

Her journey began when her father, Edwin Singh, took her with him to the golf course after he had picked up the sport.

Edwin Singh, who grew up in Naboro and left Fiji with his family at the age of 13, says as a baby, Gianna would sit in her car seat and watch him practise at the driving range and once she learned to walk, she began holding and swinging a golf club and has continued playing ever since.

Gianna started competing at around six and a half years old and has now spent more than 11 years in competitive golf.

She has played in more than 150 tournaments and has either won or reached the podium in more than half of them.

Gianna won the Optimist Junior World Championship at the age of 13 and has twice placed in the top five at the IMG Junior World Championship. She also set a California high school record by shooting 60, or 12 under par, in a league match.

Her other achievements include winning the 2024 AJGA Colorado Championship, the 2025 California Women’s State Fair Championship and the 2026 San Francisco Women’s Championship. She also finished second at the 2025 California Women’s Championship, third at the 2025 Dixie Women’s Amateur and third at the 2026 Arizona Women’s Amateur.

Gianna is also a three-time NorCal Junior Cup Series champion, has reached 1,194 in the World Amateur Golf Ranking a nd has qualified for the U.S. Women’s Amateur in Tennessee.

She also competes on the Underrated Golf Tour associated with Stephen Curry.

According to her father, Gianna has earned a full scholarship to play golf at Grand Canyon University.

Gianna says switching to homeschooling in Grade 10 helped her balance schoolwork with frequent travel for golf and better prepared her for college, where she will need to manage academics while competing with her team.

She says it's been very important for her to have all the support from her parents because she understands that not every child has the support from their parents.

Gianna says she is grateful that her parents work hard to provide for her and give her the best of the best, training and coaching to make her the best golfer she can be and to achieve her dreams .

Her father further says that Gianna’s golf journey requires significant financial commitment, where they prioritise coaching, equipment, tournament fees and travel over family holidays and other expenses.

He says Gianna also works closely with long-time PGA-certified coach Noah Montgomery to strengthen her game and mental resilience during difficult periods while physical preparation is equally important, with Gianna training in the gym under Charlie and Arnie Zamora to build the strength, fitness and consistency needed for high-level competition.

Singh says he and his wife make sure that she has everything.

HINDI INSERT: Edwin Singh on support 7 jul

Gianna further says playing alongside Fijian golf legend Vijay Singh at the 2024 PURE Insurance Championship at Pebble Beach was an eye-opening experience, particularly seeing his work ethic up close.

She says while warming up for the first day of the tournament, she saw Singh at the end of the range with about three bags of balls and he was hitting them at the end of the other side of the range without once getting tired.

Her father also says Vijay Singh’s journey from Fiji to the international stage should serve as an inspiration to young athletes.

He says what impressed him most was Singh’s commitment to the game, even after decades at the highest level and being close to 60 years old, he works harder than some of the young kids he knows.

Singh says the former world number one was humble and generous with his time during the three days they played together.

He says those lessons continue to shape the message he gives his daughter as she works towards the next level.

Gianna says next month will be her first visit to Fiji, and she hopes to use the trip to connect with and encourage young people through sport.

The young golfer is encouraging young girls to believe in themselves, dream big and work hard, saying success takes time and perseverance.

She adds that challenges and setbacks are part of the journey, but staying dedicated, trusting the process and never giving up can lead to success.

Her father says the family hopes to visit a golf course and, if possible, organise a small event for children where Gianna can help and inspire them.

Singh adds the trip will also be an opportunity to show Gianna where he was born and reconnect with family in Fiji.

At just 17, Gianna Singh hopes to represent Fiji and the United States in golf and become the first female American Info-Fijian inducted into the World Golf Hall of Fame. With more than 150 tournaments behind her, she is preparing for her first U.S Women's Amateur Championship and will visit Fiji for the first time next month.

item.title: Argentina and Egypt clash for quarter-final spot
item.description: Two of world football's biggest stars will go head-to-head tomorrow morning when Lionel Messi's Argentina take on Mohamed Salah's

item.title: Belgium thrash USA 4-1 to book World Cup quarter-final against Spain
item.description: Belgium have thrashed co-hosts the USA 4-1 in their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash to book a quarter-final showdown with Spain.C

item.title: De Ketelaere double gives Belgium 2-1 advantage over USA at halftime
item.description: Belgium head into the halftime break with a 2-1 lead over the United States in their FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash in Seattle S
